execution_context.mode_reason: Generic fixture uses the integrated GA4 workbook generator without client-specific artifacts.

preservation_requirements: Keep the five core human sheets stable and include Screenshot Register only when screenshots are requested.
allowed_changes: Populate rows and screenshot evidence rows from the structured plan.

document.title: Generic GA4 ecommerce homepage tracking plan
document.notes: Generic fixture for validating the skill contract, workbook generator, and analyst-readable output.

measurement_brief.scope: Single ecommerce homepage view and the primary discovery interactions visible on the page.
measurement_brief.business_goal: Understand how the homepage supports product discovery, merchandising exposure, and account access intent.
analysis_needs: Identify search usage and search themes
Understand which modules or navigation areas drive deeper browsing
constraints: Keep ecommerce promotion events in official GA4 ecommerce format
Normalize controlled values to lowercase ASCII snake_case
open_questions: Confirm the final promotion metadata available in the CMS.
Confirm whether account access should become a key event.

page_roles.business_purpose: Introduce merchandising, search, navigation, and account-access paths before product or checkout journeys.
page_roles.primary_success_signal: Users continue through promotion, search, content/navigation, or account-access intent.

selected_event_families.reason: The page view frames all downstream homepage interactions and supports acquisition and landing-page reporting.
Homepage promotions are merchandising objects and should use GA4 recommended ecommerce promotion events when promotion metadata is available.
Search submission expresses product or content demand and maps directly to the GA4 recommended search event.
Homepage modules and navigation links need one consolidated official event with controlled content parameters.
A pre-login account entry click is useful demand signal, while GA4 login should only fire after successful authentication.

excluded_event_families.reason: The fixture covers the homepage only; cart, checkout, transaction, and refund events need their own journeys and data.

excluded_event_families.family_name: One event per visual link or banner
excluded_event_families.reason: Repeated homepage interactions are consolidated into official promotion or content-selection events with controlled values.

custom_event_acceptance.business_reason: Measure account demand before authentication success without misusing the official login or sign_up events.
custom_event_acceptance.registration_notes: Register cta_location and cta_text only if account-entry performance is needed in GA4 reports or audiences.

lead_event_model.rationale: The selected homepage fixture has no completed lead-generation outcome.

scalability_notes: Reuse the same homepage discovery event families for future home, landing, or campaign-entry pages when the trigger and parameter structure match.
Use controlled lowercase ASCII snake_case values for component, content, and CTA parameters so future French labels remain reportable.

website_coverage_map.coverage_summary: Fixture scope covers one representative ecommerce homepage and its primary discovery interactions only.

sources_checked.used_for: Define the homepage scope, available metadata, and expected user actions.
Represent the homepage template and visible discovery components.

journeys_covered.notes: Cart, checkout, and product-listing journeys are intentionally outside this fixture.

coverage_gaps.gap: Full ecommerce catalog, cart, checkout, purchase, and refund journeys are not in fixture scope.
coverage_gaps.impact: Do not reuse this fixture alone as a whole-site ecommerce plan.
coverage_gaps.recommended_next_step: Add separate journeys and coverage evidence for PLP, PDP, cart, checkout, confirmation, and refund flows.

authenticated_journey.gap_reason: Authentication and customer-space pages are outside the selected homepage fixture scope.

journeys_discovered.decision_reason: Cart, checkout, and product-listing journeys are intentionally outside this fixture.

playwright_mcp_attempt.detail: The source plan marked live browser exploration as not needed.

browser_exploration.detail: The fixture validates a selected homepage template, not a dynamic whole-site crawl.

push_timing: initial page load when authentication state is known

ga4_user_id.mapping_rule: Map only to the Google tag user_id setting. Do not send it as an event parameter, user property, or custom dimension.

advertising_user_data.handling_rule: Direct identifiers are not sent to GA4. Define them only in a separate advertising implementation when explicitly required and approved.

events.business_question: How many users view the homepage and from which acquisition contexts?
events.trigger: Homepage load or SPA route change to the homepage.
data_dependencies: page template from CMS or dataLayer

data_layer.mapping_notes: If GA4 automatic page_view is sufficient, do not duplicate it. Use manual page_view only for controlled SPA routing or enriched page context.

privacy.notes: Remove sensitive query parameters before sending page_location if the website can contain them.

events.implementation_notes: Avoid duplicate page_view if enhanced measurement already sends the correct event.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event semantics and required parameter scope checked for page_view.
official_verification.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Page views > page_view
official_verification.trigger_source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Page views > page_view
official_verification.canonical_wording: This event signifies that the page loaded or the active site changed the browser history state.
official_verification.canonical_trigger_wording: Each time the page loads or the browser history state is changed by the active site.

duplicate_risk.reason: GA4 may collect this natively depending on stream settings.
duplicate_risk.dedupe_rule: Do not add a manual GTM duplicate unless native collection is disabled or insufficient.

events.analysis_use: How many users view the homepage and from which acquisition contexts?
events.event_summary: This event signifies that the page loaded or the active site changed the browser history state.

parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: Segments traffic and QA by the viewed URL after sensitive query parameters are removed.
Supports page-level reporting and DebugView validation with a readable page label.
Supports navigation and acquisition context analysis for page views.
Segments homepage behavior by page template for journey analysis.
Segments behavior by the active website language across page and interaction events.
Separates production traffic from staging or development data during analysis and implementation review.

events.business_question: Which homepage promotions are exposed to users?
events.trigger: Promotion module is visible according to the agreed impression threshold.
data_dependencies: promotion ID from CMS or merchandising system
promotion item ID or item name when available

data_layer.mapping_notes: Rows in the tracking plan use official GA4 parameter names. ecommerce.* is only the GTM source object.

privacy.notes: Promotion values should be controlled campaign or merchandising identifiers.

events.implementation_notes: Use one consolidated view_promotion definition and list allowed promotion values rather than one event per banner.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 ecommerce event and event-level parameter scope checked for view_promotion.
official_verification.canonical_wording: This event signifies a promotion was viewed from a list.
official_verification.trigger_source_locator: Instruction immediately preceding the view_promotion implementation example
official_verification.canonical_trigger_wording: Promotion impressions are typically measured with the initial screen view by sending the view_promotion event with an items parameter to specify the promoted item.

duplicate_risk.reason: Manual dataLayer collection is scoped to this explicit business interaction.
duplicate_risk.dedupe_rule: Fire once per qualifying user action and avoid parallel automatic or enhanced-measurement collection.

events.analysis_use: Which homepage promotions are exposed to users?
events.event_summary: This event signifies a promotion was viewed from a list.

parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: Selected for the documented analysis use of view_promotion.
Selected for the documented analysis use of view_promotion.
Selected for the documented analysis use of view_promotion.
Selected for the documented analysis use of view_promotion.
GA4 marks this parameter as required for view_promotion: The items for the event.

parameter_bindings.condition: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.
parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: GA4 marks this parameter as conditional for view_promotion: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameter_bindings.condition: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.
parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: GA4 marks this parameter as conditional for view_promotion: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

events.page_or_component: Homepage merchandising promotion link or card
events.trigger: User clicks a tracked homepage promotion.
data_dependencies: clicked promotion ID from CMS or data attributes
promotion item ID or item name when available

data_layer.mapping_notes: Use the same promotion identifiers as view_promotion for impression-to-click analysis.

privacy.notes: Use stable controlled values; avoid visible text when it changes often.

events.implementation_notes: Keep this as an ecommerce promotion event, not a generic click event.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 ecommerce event and event-level parameter scope checked for select_promotion.
official_verification.canonical_wording: This event signifies a promotion was selected from a list.
official_verification.trigger_source_locator: Instruction immediately preceding the select_promotion implementation example
official_verification.canonical_trigger_wording: To indicate a user clicked on a promotion, send a select_promotion event with that item as an item parameter.

duplicate_risk.reason: Manual dataLayer collection is scoped to this explicit business interaction.
duplicate_risk.dedupe_rule: Fire once per qualifying user action and avoid parallel automatic or enhanced-measurement collection.

events.event_summary: This event signifies a promotion was selected from a list.

parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: Selected for the documented analysis use of select_promotion.
Selected for the documented analysis use of select_promotion.
Selected for the documented analysis use of select_promotion.
Selected for the documented analysis use of select_promotion.
Selected for the documented analysis use of select_promotion.

parameter_bindings.condition: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.
parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: GA4 marks this parameter as conditional for select_promotion: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

parameter_bindings.condition: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.
parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: GA4 marks this parameter as conditional for select_promotion: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

events.business_question: What do users search for from the homepage?
events.trigger: User submits the site search form.
data_dependencies: submitted search term after PII scrubbing

data_layer.mapping_notes: Send event_data.search_term after PII scrubbing and map page.page_template without renaming either parameter.

privacy.notes: Search terms can contain PII or high-cardinality data. Scrub email, phone, order IDs, and sensitive values before sending. Do not register raw search_term as a custom dimension unless a governed reporting need is approved.

events.implementation_notes: Use the official recommended search event instead of a custom search_submit event.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event semantics and required parameter scope checked for search.
official_verification.canonical_wording: Log this event to indicate when the user has performed a search. You can use this event to identify what users are searching for on your website or app. For example, you could send this event when a user views a search results page after performing a search.
official_verification.canonical_trigger_wording: Log this event to indicate when the user has performed a search. You can use this event to identify what users are searching for on your website or app. For example, you could send this event when a user views a search results page after performing a search.

duplicate_risk.reason: Site-search can be configured through enhanced measurement or manual dataLayer tracking.
duplicate_risk.dedupe_rule: Use this dataLayer search event only when enhanced measurement site-search is disabled or insufficient for required parameters.

events.analysis_use: What do users search for from the homepage?
events.event_summary: Log this event to indicate when the user has performed a search. You can use this event to identify what users are searching for on your website or app. For example, you could send this event when a user views a search results page after performing a search.

parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: GA4 marks this parameter as required for search: The term that was searched for.
Segments homepage behavior by page template for journey analysis.

events.page_or_component: Homepage navigation, editorial, or service module
events.business_question: Which homepage modules or navigation paths drive user interest?
events.trigger: User clicks a consolidated navigation or content module.

data_layer.mapping_notes: Group similar navigation/content clicks into one select_content event with controlled values.

privacy.notes: Remove sensitive query parameters from link_url and keep controlled content names stable.

events.implementation_notes: Do not create one custom event per homepage link when this consolidated event is sufficient.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event semantics and required parameter scope checked for select_content.
official_verification.canonical_wording: This event signifies that a user has selected some content of a certain type. This event can help you identify popular content and categories of content on your website or app.
official_verification.canonical_trigger_wording: This event signifies that a user has selected some content of a certain type. This event can help you identify popular content and categories of content on your website or app.

duplicate_risk.reason: Manual dataLayer collection is scoped to this explicit business interaction.
duplicate_risk.dedupe_rule: Fire once per qualifying user action and avoid parallel automatic or enhanced-measurement collection.

events.analysis_use: Which homepage modules or navigation paths drive user interest?
events.event_summary: This event signifies that a user has selected some content of a certain type. This event can help you identify popular content and categories of content on your website or app.

parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: Selected for the documented analysis use of select_content.
Selected for the documented analysis use of select_content.
Provides readable selected-content labels for analyst review while managing cardinality.
Compares CTA performance by page area or component location.
Validates navigation destination and supports outbound or internal path analysis after URL cleaning.

events.official_match: custom GA4 event justified by account entry intent before login
events.business_question: How often do users try to access the account area from the homepage?
events.trigger: User clicks the account access entry point before the login result is known.
data_dependencies: approved login status value when available

data_layer.mapping_notes: Use the official login event only when a login actually succeeds.

privacy.notes: Do not send email, account ID, phone number, or customer number.

events.implementation_notes: This is custom because the official login event should represent successful authentication, not an account-link click.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom GA4 event; official alternatives are documented in custom_event_acceptance.

duplicate_risk.reason: Manual dataLayer collection is scoped to this explicit business interaction.
duplicate_risk.dedupe_rule: Fire once per qualifying user action and avoid parallel automatic or enhanced-measurement collection.

events.analysis_use: How often do users try to access the account area from the homepage?
events.event_summary: This event signifies that a user clicks the account access entry point before the login result is known.

parameter_bindings.inclusion_reason: Compares CTA performance by page area or component location.
Supports account-access intent analysis by comparing CTA wording without collecting personal data.

parameters.description: The complete URL of the webpage that someone visited on your website.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Segments traffic and QA by the viewed URL after sensitive query parameters are removed.
parameters.value_rules: Use the current URL after removing sensitive query parameters.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event parameter scope checked for page_location.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The complete URL of the webpage that someone visited on your website.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The HTML page title that you set on your website. The page_title parameter can override the title HTML tag.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports page-level reporting and DebugView validation with a readable page label.
parameters.value_rules: Use the page title generated by the CMS or browser.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event parameter scope checked for page_title.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The HTML page title that you set on your website. The page_title parameter can override the title HTML tag.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The referring URL, which is the user's previous URL and can be your website's domain or other domains.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports navigation and acquisition context analysis for page views.
parameters.value_rules: Use browser referrer after removing sensitive query parameters when possible.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event parameter scope checked for page_referrer.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The referring URL, which is the user's previous URL and can be your website's domain or other domains.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The stable CMS or frontend template used to render the current page or virtual route.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Segments homepage behavior by page template for journey analysis.
parameters.official_gap: Official page_location and page_title fields identify the page but do not provide a stable CMS or frontend template classification.
parameters.value_rules: Use controlled lowercase ASCII snake_case values.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom page_template event parameter; not an official GA4 parameter.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The website navigation and content language selected when the event occurs.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Segments behavior by the active website language across page and interaction events.
parameters.official_gap: GA4's automatically collected language describes the user's browser or device language, not necessarily the active website content language.
parameters.value_rules: Use a lowercase ISO 639-1 language code observed in the website selector or locale route.
parameters.source: Website language selector or locale route

official_verification.scope_note: Custom page-context parameter using a standard language-code rule.

value_domain.notes: The generic fixture proposes one language value; a real plan must inspect the website selector and locale routes.

parameters.description: The website environment in which the event is generated.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Separates production traffic from staging or development data during analysis and implementation review.
parameters.official_gap: The reviewed GA4 page and event fields do not identify the application's deployment environment.
parameters.value_rules: Use the controlled deployment environment name supplied by the application configuration.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom page-context parameter for deployment environment governance.

value_domain.notes: The generic fixture proposes common environment values; a real plan must confirm the deployment taxonomy.

parameters.description: The ID of the promotion associated with the event.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Joins promotion exposure and click performance using a stable merchandising identifier.
parameters.value_rules: Use a stable campaign or merchandising ID shared by impression and click.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 ecommerce event parameter scope checked for promotion_id.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The ID of the promotion associated with the event.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The name of the promotion associated with the event.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports readable promotion performance reporting and merchandising review.
parameters.value_rules: Use normalized controlled values with accents removed.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 ecommerce event parameter scope checked for promotion_name.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The name of the promotion associated with the event.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The name of the promotional creative.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Compares promotion creative variants in exposure and click analysis.
parameters.value_rules: Use stable creative naming.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 ecommerce event parameter scope checked for creative_name.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The name of the promotional creative.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The name of the promotional creative slot associated with the event.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Compares promotion placement performance across homepage areas.
parameters.value_rules: Use section and position.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 ecommerce event parameter scope checked for creative_slot.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The name of the promotional creative slot associated with the event.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The items for the event.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Carries the ecommerce item collection required for product and promotion analysis.
parameters.value_rules: Include at least one item object when ecommerce promotion or item context is available.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 ecommerce event parameter scope checked for items.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Identifies products or offers consistently across ecommerce and promotion events.
parameters.value_rules: One of items[].item_id or items[].item_name is required when items is sent.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 item-scoped ecommerce parameter scope checked for items[].item_id.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The ID of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Provides readable product or offer labels for ecommerce validation and reporting.
parameters.value_rules: One of items[].item_id or items[].item_name is required when items is sent.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 item-scoped ecommerce parameter scope checked for items[].item_name.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The name of the item. *One of item_id or item_name is required.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The term that was searched for.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Analyzes onsite search demand after PII scrubbing and supports search journey optimization.
parameters.value_rules: Scrub direct PII and sensitive terms before sending.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event parameter scope checked for search_term.
official_verification.canonical_wording: The term that was searched for.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: The type of selected content.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Segments consolidated content selections by component or content family.
parameters.value_rules: Use controlled lowercase ASCII snake_case values.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event parameter scope checked for content_type.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: An identifier for the content that was selected. The value must include at least one non-numeric character.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Identifies selected content with a stable low-cardinality ID for module performance reporting.
parameters.value_rules: Use stable IDs or slugs.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 event parameter scope checked for content_id.
official_verification.canonical_wording: An identifier for the content that was selected. The value must include at least one non-numeric character.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: Readable selected content name.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Provides readable selected-content labels for analyst review while managing cardinality.
parameters.official_gap: The official select_content fields provide content_type and content_id but no separate governed human-readable content name.
parameters.value_rules: Use normalized lowercase ASCII snake_case values when controlled.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom readable content label retained only for the stated reporting need.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: Location of a clicked CTA or module.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Compares CTA performance by page area or component location.
parameters.official_gap: The reviewed official event fields identify selected content but do not describe the page area or component location of the CTA.
parameters.value_rules: Use controlled lowercase ASCII snake_case values.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom analytics parameter cta_location; governed by GA4 naming and registration rules.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: Destination URL for a selected link or module.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Validates navigation destination and supports outbound or internal path analysis after URL cleaning.
parameters.official_gap: The official select_content fields do not provide a destination URL; enhanced-measurement link_url belongs to a different click collection surface.
parameters.value_rules: Remove sensitive query parameters before sending.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom analytics parameter link_url; governed by GA4 naming and registration rules.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: Normalized visible CTA text.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports account-access intent analysis by comparing CTA wording without collecting personal data.
parameters.official_gap: The reviewed login and sign_up fields describe successful authentication methods, not the governed label of a pre-authentication account CTA.
parameters.value_rules: Use lowercase ASCII snake_case controlled values and remove accents.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom analytics parameter cta_text; governed by GA4 naming and registration rules.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: Opaque stable authenticated identifier mapped only to the GA4 User-ID configuration setting.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports cross-session and cross-device signed-in journey analysis through GA4 User-ID.
parameters.value_rules: Use a stable non-PII internal identifier. Omit before sign-in and set null after logout. Never use email or register this value as a custom dimension.

official_verification.scope_note: Official GA4 User-ID configuration behavior and logout clearing rule verified.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: Known anonymous login state.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Segments GA4 users and journeys by the current authenticated state.
parameters.official_gap: GA4 User-ID provides an opaque identity configuration but no predefined low-cardinality signed-in versus signed-out user property.
parameters.value_rules: Use logged_in | logged_out and update immediately after login or logout.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom GA4 user property governed by official user-property behavior and registration rules.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: Low-cardinality customer lifecycle group available after authentication.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Segments authenticated journey and retention analysis by new or returning customer status.
parameters.official_gap: The purchase customer_type parameter is event-specific and does not provide a governed current-customer user property across authenticated journeys.
parameters.value_rules: Use new | returning | unknown from an approved backend rule.

official_verification.scope_note: Custom GA4 user property with project-defined controlled values.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

parameters.description: Optional low-cardinality account category when the business has stable account types.
parameters.reporting_purpose: Supports authenticated journey segmentation by a governed account category.
parameters.official_gap: The reviewed GA4 user and event fields do not provide a predefined business account-category property.
parameters.value_rules: Use a small English controlled taxonomy defined by the business; omit when no meaningful account categories exist.

official_verification.scope_note: Optional custom GA4 user property with project-defined controlled values.

value_domain.notes: Migrated from the existing value rules; verify the value source and completeness before publication.

not_tracked.reason: Hover interactions are noisy, not reliable on mobile, and do not answer the stated analysis needs.

not_tracked.interaction: Every homepage link as a separate custom event
not_tracked.reason: A consolidated select_content event with controlled values is easier to maintain and analyze.

assumptions: The page is an ecommerce homepage but no product list, cart, or checkout context is guaranteed from this single-page scope.
Promotion metadata is available from the CMS or merchandising layer.
Controlled analytics values are normalized to lowercase ASCII snake_case with accents removed.
